reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  栈满条件错误，应为S->top==MAX-1
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The condition for stack full is incorrect. With MAX=101 and indices 0-100, the stack is full when top == MAX-1 (100). Checking MAX-2 (99) causes an error when pushing the 100th element. It should be S->top == MAX - 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The maximum capacity is 100 (indices 0-99). The check should be S->top == MAX - 1 (or 99) to detect when the stack is full before pushing. Using MAX-2 causes an "error" to be returned when there is still one slot available, and potentially allows overflow if not handled correctly elsewhere, though here it just returns -1 prematurely. More importantly, the problem states max capacity 100. If top is 99, it's full. So check should be S->top >= MAX - 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The condition should be S->top == MAX - 1 because the stack size is MAX (101), valid indices are 0 to 100. When top is 99 (MAX-2), there's still room for one more element. Error occurs when trying to push beyond index 100.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // The condition should be S->top == MAX - 1 because the stack has MAX capacity (indices 0 to MAX-1). Using MAX-2 incorrectly reduces capacity by 1.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Similar to above, the output format requires "error " with a space, not a newline. It should be printf("error ");
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The problem requires outputting "error " (with a space) and all outputs on a single line separated by spaces. Using \n breaks the single-line requirement.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Output format requires a space after each element/error, not a newline.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: should output "%d " (with a trailing space, no newline) to produce space-separated output on a single line.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The output format requires all popped elements on a single line separated by spaces. Using \n prints each element on a new line. It should be printf("%d ", q);
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The problem requires outputting elements separated by spaces on a single line. Using \n prints each element on a new line. It should be printf("%d ", q);
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // Should print "%d " with a space, not newline, to match expected output format.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // Output format requires a space after each element/error, not a newline.

663.reason: The loop termination condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It will rarely return -1 (EOF) in this context as intended. The loop should continue until jud is -1, checked inside the loop.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  The loop termination condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It will rarely return -1 (EOF) in this context as intended. The loop should continue until jud is -1, checked inside the loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  scanf returns the number of items successfully read. It does not return -1 at EOF typically (it returns EOF which is usually -1, but the loop condition logic is fragile). However, the bigger issue is the output format.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should be while(scanf("%d", &jud) == 1). Also, the problem requires stopping when -1 is read, but this loop never checks for -1 as a termination condition properly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  // The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. It should check for EOF or compare jud after reading. Also, the problem requires stopping at -1, but this loop never breaks on jud == -1.
